tools.name: list_agents
tools.description: List the agents you can target with create_thread. Returns each agent's id, name, description, and executionMode (`auto`, or `confirm` for Ask first). Agents with `executionMode: "confirm"` are listed so you can see them. While Ask-first enforcement is enabled, create_thread refuses them because MCP has no approval surface; otherwise they run with Auto behavior. If refused, run them in Hyperagent or switch the agent to Auto.

tools.name: create_attachment_upload
tools.description: Begin attaching a file to a thread without reading its bytes into your context. Returns a fileId, a short-lived presigned uploadUrl, and requiredHeaders. Upload the file's bytes straight to uploadUrl with an HTTP PUT, sending every header in requiredHeaders (e.g. `curl -T path/to/file -H "If-None-Match: *" <uploadUrl>`) — never read the file's contents yourself. Then pass the fileId in create_thread / send_message attachmentIds. This keeps large files (images, PDFs) entirely out of your token budget.

mimeType.description: MIME type, e.g. image/png or application/pdf

sizeBytes.description: Exact file size in bytes (e.g. from stat). Rejected up front if over the per-file cap, and the upload must match it exactly.

tools.name: create_thread
tools.description: Start a new hyperagent thread running one of the user's agents and return its threadId immediately while the agent works in the background. The message is the thread's opening user message — make it self-contained. Poll get_thread until isRunning is false, then read the final messages for the result. To attach a file: (1) call create_attachment_upload to get uploadUrl+fileId (plus any requiredHeaders), (2) upload the bytes to uploadUrl with an HTTP PUT, sending every header in requiredHeaders (e.g. `curl -T file -H "If-None-Match: *" <uploadUrl>`) — do NOT read the file into your context, (3) pass fileId in attachmentIds. This keeps large files out of your token budget.

message.description: Opening user message for the new thread

namingHint.description: Optional hint for auto-naming the thread

attachmentIds.description: fileIds returned by create_attachment_upload, after you've uploaded each file's bytes to its uploadUrl. Attach files this way to keep their bytes out of your token budget.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a follow-up message to an existing thread. The message is queued as the thread's next user turn — immediately if idle, after the current turn if busy. Poll get_thread for the response. To attach a file: (1) call create_attachment_upload to get uploadUrl+fileId (plus any requiredHeaders), (2) upload the bytes to uploadUrl with an HTTP PUT, sending every header in requiredHeaders (e.g. `curl -T file -H "If-None-Match: *" <uploadUrl>`) — do NOT read the file into your context, (3) pass fileId in attachmentIds. This keeps large files out of your token budget.

message.description: Message to queue as the thread's next turn

attachmentIds.description: fileIds returned by create_attachment_upload, after you've uploaded each file's bytes to its uploadUrl. Attach files this way to keep their bytes out of your token budget.

tools.name: get_thread
tools.description: Read a thread: recent messages (oldest-first window) plus a live isRunning flag. While isRunning is true the agent is still working — wait before polling again. When it flips false, the last assistant message is the result.

messageLimit.description: Max recent messages to return (default 50, max 200)

tools.name: list_threads
tools.description: List the user's threads, newest first, keyset-paginated. Returns thread summaries and a nextCursor to pass back for the following page.
